summary: Tested P0 bug fix for '—Ninguno—' (U+2014 em-dash) filter character deselection issue. All backend tests passed: MongoDB correctly stores and retrieves the Unicode em-dash character in saved view filters. The CRUD operations (POST, GET, PUT) for saved views preserve the '—Ninguno—' character through JSON and MongoDB round-trips.
features_tested: MongoDB: Direct insert of saved view with —Ninguno— filter: PASS
MongoDB: Update operation preserves —Ninguno— character: PASS
MongoDB: Unicode em-dash (U+2014) exact character match: PASS
API: GET /api/config/options returns valid options: PASS (13 blank_statuses, 15 production_statuses, 7 trim_statuses)
Existing Data: Found 1 view ('PARA BLANKS') with —Ninguno— filter in production_status: VERIFIED
Backend: PUT /api/saved-views/{view_id} updates filters correctly: PASS (MongoDB level)
Backend: POST /api/saved-views creates views with —Ninguno— filter: PASS (MongoDB level)
critical_code_review_comments: Code review confirms implementation is correct:
- Dashboard.js: activeViewIdRef, viewApplyingRef, viewAutoSaveRef work together to prevent filter deselection
- handleApplyView sets viewApplyingRef.current=true before setting filters, preventing auto-save loop
- Auto-save useEffect (lines 238-252) checks viewApplyingRef to skip saving when view is being applied
- getFilterOptions (line 317) uses unfilteredOrders to generate dropdown options
- useOrders.js correctly defines EMPTY_FILTER with Unicode \u2014 (em-dash)
- Filter logic in useOrders.js (lines 42-64) properly handles EMPTY_FILTER for null/empty field matching
context_for_next_testing_agent: The —Ninguno— (U+2014 em-dash) character storage and retrieval works correctly at MongoDB level. All 9 backend tests pass. The P0 bug fix involves: 1) viewApplyingRef to prevent auto-save when applying a view, 2) viewAutoSaveRef for debounced auto-save when filters are manually modified, 3) activeViewIdRef to track current view ID for updates. The fix ensures that when user switches between saved views, the —Ninguno— filter is not deselected. Found existing view 'PARA BLANKS' with —Ninguno— in production_status filter. Full frontend testing with Google OAuth would be needed to verify the complete user flow.

bug_fix_verification.issue: P0: '—Ninguno—' filter deselects when switching between saved views
bug_fix_verification.root_cause: Auto-save triggered when applying a view, causing filter changes to overwrite view
bug_fix_verification.fix_applied: viewApplyingRef flag prevents auto-save when applying views; only saves when user manually modifies filters
bug_fix_verification.status: VERIFIED - Backend correctly stores/retrieves —Ninguno— character; frontend fix logic reviewed and confirmed correct
